publications.notes: Published through the shared NGINX edge and gated behind the repo-managed Keycloak oauth2-proxy flow.
The dashboard is protected by the shared edge oauth2-proxy and Keycloak flow while /js/, /api/event, and health endpoints stay public for tracker delivery and probe traffic.
Published publicly through the shared NGINX edge and verified live from current main.
Shared public applications ingress published through the NGINX edge with wildcard alias coverage for Coolify-managed applications.
Published through the shared NGINX edge while browser authentication remains service-native through the repo-managed Keycloak OIDC client.
The shared edge protects the Lago browser UI while `/api/health` and `/api/v1/events` intentionally stay reachable for probes and governed producer ingest through the API gateway adapter.

repo_route_metadata.title: Docker Build VM
repo_route_metadata.description: The build VM is a private operator-facing host for interactive build workflows and is not a public web workload.
repo_route_metadata.meta: Reach the build VM through the approved operator access path rather than the public edge.

publications.notes: Published through the shared NGINX edge and gated behind the repo-managed Keycloak oauth2-proxy flow.
ServerClaw is published through the shared NGINX edge and delegates browser authentication to its in-app Keycloak OIDC flow.
Published through the shared NGINX edge and authenticated through Woodpecker's delegated Gitea OAuth flow.
Published through the shared NGINX edge while Nextcloud itself enforces browser and DAV authentication plus .well-known DAV discovery redirects.
Coolify dashboard published through the shared authenticated NGINX edge with the private controller API proxied on the management tailnet.
The shared NGINX edge publishes data.lv3.org directly to Directus. Browser users authenticate upstream through Keycloak and API consumers authenticate with Directus-issued bearer tokens.
Tailnet-only TCP proxy through the Proxmox host.

repo_route_metadata.title: Docker Runtime VM
repo_route_metadata.description: The Docker runtime VM does not currently publish a public web service at this hostname.
repo_route_metadata.meta: Public application endpoints should be published deliberately through the NGINX edge when needed.

publications.notes: Published through the shared NGINX edge and gated behind the repo-managed Keycloak oauth2-proxy flow.
Private Excalidraw publication for repo-generated architecture scenes through the shared authenticated NGINX edge.

Tailnet-only TCP proxy for the private Gitea runtime on docker-runtime-lv3.

Published through the shared NGINX edge while Grist itself delegates operator browser sign-in to the repo-managed Keycloak OIDC client.
Published through the shared NGINX edge and proxied to the Proxmox host Headscale listener on 10.10.10.1:8080.
Unified service dashboard published through the shared NGINX edge and protected by the repo-managed Keycloak oauth2-proxy flow.
The shared NGINX edge proxies signalling at https://livekit.lv3.org while public TCP 7881 and UDP 7882 are forwarded directly to docker-runtime-lv3 for WebRTC media.
Published through the shared NGINX edge and gated behind the repo-managed Keycloak oauth2-proxy flow.
The apex hostname is governed as a crawl-suppressed informational redirect to the shared NGINX edge entrypoint.

repo_route_metadata.title: LV3 Mail
repo_route_metadata.description: The managed LV3 mail service accepts SMTP and IMAPS traffic on the published mail hostname without exposing a browser-facing mailbox UI.
repo_route_metadata.meta: Use the declared mail protocols on ports 587 and 993 instead of expecting a public web application at this hostname.

publications.notes: Published as an informational HTTPS surface on the shared edge while the live mail service itself is reached over SMTP submission and IMAPS.
The public hostname terminates at the shared edge and forwards only the Matrix client listener; authentication is enforced by Synapse and federation stays disabled for this ADR.
The MinIO browser console is published through the shared authenticated edge path and proxied to the docker-runtime guest listener on port 9011.
The public MinIO S3-compatible API is published through the shared NGINX edge and proxies to the docker-runtime guest listener on port 9010.
The editor is protected by the shared oauth2-proxy and Keycloak edge flow while /healthz and /webhook* remain intentionally unauthenticated for machine callers.

repo_route_metadata.title: LV3 Edge
repo_route_metadata.description: NGINX is the public edge for published lv3.org services on this platform.
repo_route_metadata.meta: Use the named subdomains for the actual service entry points exposed at the edge.

Interactive runtime is live behind oauth2-proxy and Keycloak on the shared NGINX edge.
Planned staging portal generated from the canonical service and environment catalogs.
Published through the shared NGINX edge; docs.lv3.org remains reserved for the developer portal from ADR 0094.

repo_route_metadata.title: Proxmox Administration
repo_route_metadata.description: Proxmox administration remains private and is reached over Tailscale instead of being published directly on the public edge.
repo_route_metadata.meta: Use Tailscale host access for routine Proxmox administration.
